title: Signals and Systems - Delta Function Problems
summary: This page contains practice problems related to the Dirac delta function, including evaluating integrals involving delta functions, sifting properties, and generalized function definitions. Problems cover delta function composition with various signals, convolution integrals, and a proof involving the derivative of the delta function using integration by parts.

figures.caption: Signals for problem 1.4-9
figures.description: The figure has two time-domain plots with horizontal axis labeled t and vertical amplitude markings at 1 and -1. In (a), the signal is piecewise constant: it is 0 before t=0, equals 1 from t=0 to t=1, equals -1 from t=1 to t=3, then returns to 0 for t>3; a circled upward arrow at t=3 indicates a positive jump of magnitude 1. In (b), the signal is a constant level of 1 for all shown t, with downward impulse arrows at t=1, t=2, and t=3, each labeled -1. Students should notice the jump discontinuities and the correspondence between step changes in (a) and impulse events superimposed on the constant signal in (b).
